html:
[203]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 至人 - Refers to a sage or enlightened person, often used in Daoist and Confucian contexts to denote someone of extraordinary virtue or wisdom.
  • 精专孝行 - Means 'diligent and focused on filial piety,' emphasizing the depth and dedication of the virtue.
  • 感动乾坤 - Literally 'moved the heavens and earth,' a hyperbolic expression indicating a profound impact on the natural and moral order.
  • 斗中真人 - Refers to a true person or sage who resides in the 'Dǒu' (斗), which could be interpreted as a star constellation or a spiritual realm.
  • 孝弟王 - '孝' means filial piety, and '弟' means fraternal respect, thus '孝弟王' refers to a king who embodies these virtues.
  • 玉清三天 - Refers to the three heavens or realms in Daoist cosmology, often associated with the highest level of spiritual purity and enlightenment.
  • 太清 - Refers to the highest level of the Daoist triad, representing the purest form of the Dao.
  • 虚无自然 - Means 'empty and natural,' a Daoist concept emphasizing the state of being in harmony with the Dao.
  • 玉皇 - Refers to the Jade Emperor, the supreme deity in Daoism.
  • 至道秘防 - Means 'the highest secret teachings' or 'esoteric doctrines,' referring to profound and hidden knowledge.
  • 颖悟真机 - Means 'quickly understands the true essence,' indicating a deep and immediate grasp of spiritual truths.
  • 黙辨往由 - Means 'silently discerns the past causes,' suggesting a deep understanding of the origins and reasons behind events.
  • 顾知前事 - Means 'knows the past events,' indicating a clear awareness of historical or personal history.
  • 解化之坟 - Means 'tomb of transformation,' referring to a place where a being has undergone a spiritual transformation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 兖州曲阜县 - Refers to Qufu, a city in Shandong Province, famous as the birthplace of Confucius.
  • 晋代 - Refers to the Jin Dynasty (266-420 CE), a period in Chinese history known for its cultural and philosophical developments.
  • 真仙许逊 - Refers to Xu Xun, a legendary figure in Chinese mythology, often associated with Daoist immortality and wisdom.
  • 众仙之长 - Means 'the leader of the immortals,' indicating a position of great respect and authority among spiritual beings.

Proper Nouns

  • 兰公 - The name of the sage or enlightened person mentioned in the text.
  • 斗中真人 - The name of the sage who appears to Lan Gong.
  • 孝弟王 - The title given to the sage, emphasizing his virtue.
  • 玉清三天 - A term referring to the three highest heavens in Daoist cosmology.
  • 太清 - A term referring to the highest level of the Daoist triad.
  • 虚无自然 - A term describing the state of being in harmony with the Dao.
  • 玉皇 - The name of the supreme deity in Daoism.
  • 至道秘防 - A term referring to the highest secret teachings.
  • 真仙许逊 - The name of a legendary figure in Chinese mythology.
  • 众仙之长 - A title indicating the leader of the immortals.

[204]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 以何验实 - Question asking 'How can we verify?' or 'What evidence can confirm?'
  • 复形 - Literally 'restore form,' referring to the process of returning to a human or divine form.
  • 地仙 - Refers to a type of immortal who resides on Earth.
  • 长生久视 - Phrase meaning 'eternal life' or 'longevity,' often used in Daoist texts.
  • 绵养真气 - Means 'to nourish the true essence' or 'to sustain the vital energy.'
  • 轻举 - Literally 'light ascension,' referring to the act of rising into the heavens.
  • 孝道 - Refers to the Confucian concept of filial piety, emphasizing respect and care for one's parents and ancestors.
  • 化现 - Means 'manifestation' or 'appearance,' often used in religious contexts to describe divine or supernatural appearances.
  • 寳经 - Refers to a precious or sacred text.
  • 金丹 - Refers to an elixir of immortality or a spiritual pill in Daoist alchemy.
  • 高明大使 - Refers to a high-ranking official or a spiritual leader.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 地仙 - A type of immortal in Daoist mythology who lives on Earth and has achieved a form of immortality.
  • 金丹 - In Daoist alchemy, the elixir of immortality, believed to grant eternal life and spiritual enlightenment.
  • 孝道 - A core Confucian virtue emphasizing filial piety and respect for one's parents and ancestors.
  • 许真君 - Xu Xuan, a legendary figure in Daoism known for his spiritual powers and contributions to alchemy.
  • 吴都 - Refers to the ancient capital of Wu, a region in present-day Jiangsu and Zhejiang provinces.
  • 丹阳 - Refers to a place in present-day Jiangsu province, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 童子 - Refers to young boys, often used in religious contexts to describe disciples or attendants.
  • 灵孩 - Refers to a spiritual or divine child, often used in religious texts to describe a child with special spiritual significance.
  • 十二真君 - Refers to a group of twelve revered Daoist immortals or spiritual figures.
  • 樊笼 - A metaphor for the constraints of the material world, often used in Daoist and Buddhist texts to describe the cycle of birth and death.
  • 符臻至道 - Refers to the attainment of the highest spiritual path through the use of talismans and magical symbols.
  • 十二真君传 - Refers to a text or collection of texts that document the lives and teachings of the twelve revered Daoist immortals.

Proper Nouns

  • 兰公 - Refers to Lan Gong, a historical or legendary figure mentioned in the text.
  • 许真君 - Refers to Xu Xuan, a legendary figure in Daoism known for his spiritual powers and contributions to alchemy.
  • 十二真君 - Refers to a group of twelve revered Daoist immortals or spiritual figures.
  • 吴都 - Refers to the ancient capital of Wu, a region in present-day Jiangsu and Zhejiang provinces.
  • 丹阳 - Refers to a place in present-day Jiangsu province, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 童子 - Refers to young boys, often used in religious contexts to describe disciples or attendants.
  • 灵孩 - Refers to a spiritual or divine child, often used in religious texts to describe a child with special spiritual significance.
  • 樊笼 - A metaphor for the constraints of the material world, often used in Daoist and Buddhist texts to describe the cycle of birth and death.
  • 符臻至道 - Refers to the attainment of the highest spiritual path through the use of talismans and magical symbols.
  • 十二真君传 - Refers to a text or collection of texts that document the lives and teachings of the twelve revered Daoist immortals.
  • 金丹 - Refers to an elixir of immortality or a spiritual pill in Daoist alchemy.
  • 高明大使 - Refers to a high-ranking official or a spiritual leader.

[205]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 因射熊入王屋山 - Because of shooting a bear and entering Mount Wangwu. Describes the action that led to the encounter with the hermit.
  • 神状竒异 - Strange and extraordinary appearance. Describes the hermit's unusual and divine-like appearance.
  • 稽首起居 - Bowing and greeting. Describes the act of showing deep respect by bowing and greeting.
  • 可暂往观中眺望 - You can temporarily go to the temple and look around. Suggests a temporary visit to the temple for observation.
  • 心神惶怖 - Heart and spirit filled with fear and awe. Describes the intense emotional response of the person upon seeing the temple.
  • 凡夫肉人 - Ordinary mortal. Refers to the common person, emphasizing their lack of spiritual or divine status.
  • 大道 - The Great Way or Tao. Refers to the ultimate truth or principle in Daoist philosophy.
  • 天堂 - Heaven or Paradise. Refers to a divine realm or a place of spiritual enlightenment.
  • 情诚喜悦 - True and sincere joy. Describes the genuine happiness felt by the person upon encountering the divine.
  • 沈溺 - Being deeply immersed or ensnared. Describes a state of being trapped in sin or worldly desires.
  • 先身微縁 - Past life's slight karmic connection. Suggests a minimal karmic link from a previous life.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 阮基 - A name, likely a historical figure or a fictional character in the context of the story.
  • 河内 - Refers to a region in ancient China, specifically the area around the Yellow River.
  • 周武帝建徳七年 - Refers to the year 578 CE during the reign of Emperor Wu of Zhou.
  • 王屋山 - A mountain range in Henan Province, known for its religious and cultural significance.
  • 道士 - Taoist priest or hermit. Refers to a person who practices Taoism and often lives in seclusion.
  • 松树 - Pine tree, often symbolizing longevity and resilience in Chinese culture.
  • 金玉 - Gold and jade, symbols of wealth and nobility.
  • 绀碧琉璃 - A type of blue-green glass, often used to describe precious stones or decorative materials.
  • 清风时起锵然有声 - Clear wind that occasionally rises with a ringing sound, creating a serene and harmonious atmosphere.
  • 观门台殿 - Temple gates and halls, structures that are part of a religious or spiritual site.
  • 严丽 - Strict and beautiful, describing the grandeur and elegance of the temple.
  • 土地清净 - Clean and pure land, indicating a sacred and unspoiled environment.
  • 神状竒异 - Divine and strange appearance, emphasizing the otherworldly nature of the hermit.
  • 稽首 - Kneeling and bowing, a gesture of deep respect and reverence.
  • 起居 - Greeting and showing respect, a formal way of acknowledging someone.

Proper Nouns

  • 阮基 - A name, likely a historical figure or a fictional character in the context of the story.
  • 河内 - A region in ancient China, specifically the area around the Yellow River.
  • 周武帝 - Emperor Wu of Zhou, a ruler of the Northern Zhou dynasty.
  • 建徳七年 - The seventh year of Emperor Wu of Zhou's reign, corresponding to 578 CE.
  • 王屋山 - A mountain range in Henan Province, known for its religious and cultural significance.
  • 道士 - A Taoist priest or hermit.
  • 松树 - Pine tree, a common symbol in Chinese culture.
  • 金玉 - Gold and jade, symbols of wealth and nobility.
  • 绀碧琉璃 - A type of blue-green glass, often used to describe precious stones or decorative materials.
  • 清风时起锵然有声 - Clear wind that occasionally rises with a ringing sound, creating a serene and harmonious atmosphere.
  • 观门台殿 - Temple gates and halls, structures that are part of a religious or spiritual site.
  • 严丽 - Strict and beautiful, describing the grandeur and elegance of the temple.
  • 土地清净 - Clean and pure land, indicating a sacred and unspoiled environment.
  • 神状竒异 - Divine and strange appearance, emphasizing the otherworldly nature of the hermit.
  • 稽首 - Kneeling and bowing, a gesture of deep respect and reverence.

[206]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 叩头千百求乞生津 - Hyperbolic expression indicating extreme pleading or supplication; rendered as 'knocking one's head a thousand times to beg for saliva.'
  • 舍恶从善 - Moral exhortation meaning 'to abandon evil and follow good.'
  • 誓弃弓矢 - Symbolic commitment to peace or renunciation of violence, meaning 'to swear to abandon weapons.'
  • 授基智惠上品十戒 - Granting of the highest level of wisdom and the Ten Precepts, likely Buddhist precepts.
  • 设蔬食 - Preparation of vegetarian food, often associated with religious or ascetic practices.
  • 食讫令去 - Formal dismissal after eating, indicating a ritual or formal action.
  • 命绝之时 - Temporal marker meaning 'at the moment of death.'
  • 黄衣使者 - Celestial or divine figure, likely in a Buddhist or Daoist context.
  • 执文书 - Holding documents, suggesting a role in record-keeping or administration.
  • 状如台府 - Descriptive comparison meaning 'resembling a palace or government office.'
  • 大防 - Great hall or main hall, often used in religious or ceremonial contexts.
  • 阶前小吏数十人 - Dozens of small officials before the steps, indicating a hierarchical structure.
  • 积罪深厚 - Descriptive term meaning 'deeply accumulated sins.'
  • 地狱 - Place of punishment in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 仓卒惶怖 - Descriptive term meaning 'in a state of panic and fear.'
  • 圣师 - Sage teacher, likely a revered spiritual or moral guide.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 基 - Historical or literary figure who undergoes a spiritual transformation.
  • 师 - Teacher or spiritual guide, likely a figure of authority in a religious or philosophical tradition.
  • 智惠上品十戒 - Highest level of wisdom and the Ten Precepts, likely Buddhist precepts.
  • 蔬食 - Vegetarian food, often associated with ascetic or religious practices.
  • 黄衣使者 - Celestial or divine figure, likely in a Buddhist or Daoist context.
  • 台府 - Grand or official setting, possibly a palace or government office.
  • 大防 - Great hall or main hall, often used in religious or ceremonial contexts.
  • 地狱 - Place of punishment in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 圣师 - Sage teacher, likely a revered spiritual or moral guide.

Proper Nouns

  • 基 - Name of a person, likely a historical or literary figure.
  • 师 - Title of a teacher or spiritual guide.
  • 智惠上品十戒 - Title of a set of moral guidelines, likely Buddhist precepts.
  • 蔬食 - Term for vegetarian food.
  • 黄衣使者 - Title of a celestial or divine figure.
  • 台府 - Term for a grand or official setting.
  • 大防 - Term for a great hall or main hall.
  • 地狱 - Term for hell.
  • 圣师 - Title of a sage teacher.

[207]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 困幸垂救济 - Moment of divine grace or intervention, often associated with Taoist or Buddhist concepts of salvation.
  • 太上救苦经 - Sacred text believed to offer salvation and relief from suffering, often associated with Taoist or Buddhist traditions.
  • 可去勿住此深勤精进 - Command to leave and continue striving diligently, emphasizing the importance of spiritual practice.
  • 后更与汝相见 - Promise of future meeting, suggesting a continuation of the spiritual journey.
  • 失师所在唯觉香气氛氲 - Describes the disappearance of the teacher and the lingering fragrance, symbolizing the lasting impact of the encounter.
  • 黄衣使者引基至家 - Refers to a messenger in yellow robes leading the protagonist back home, often associated with divine or spiritual guidance.
  • 慎持念遂抄录传于世 - Indicates the careful preservation and dissemination of the teachings, emphasizing the importance of passing on spiritual knowledge.
  • 辞亲友入王屋山 - Describes the protagonist leaving family and friends to enter Wutai Mountain, a place of spiritual retreat and enlightenment.
  • 莫知所在 - Indicates that the protagonist's ultimate fate or whereabouts are unknown, adding an element of mystery.
  • 神仙感遇传 - The title of the source text, suggesting a narrative of divine encounters and spiritual transformations.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 困幸垂救济 - Refers to a moment of divine grace or intervention, often associated with Taoist or Buddhist concepts of salvation.
  • 圣师 - A holy teacher or sage, often associated with Taoist or Buddhist traditions.
  • 冥官 - Underworld officials, often associated with Taoist beliefs about the afterlife and the underworld.
  • 太上救苦经 - A sacred text believed to offer salvation and relief from suffering, often associated with Taoist or Buddhist traditions.
  • 王屋山 - A mountain in China known for its spiritual significance and association with Taoist and Buddhist practices.
  • 神仙感遇传 - A narrative of divine encounters and spiritual transformations, often associated with Taoist or Buddhist literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 王屋山 - A mountain in China known for its spiritual significance and association with Taoist and Buddhist practices.
  • 太上救苦经 - A sacred text believed to offer salvation and relief from suffering, often associated with Taoist or Buddhist traditions.
  • 神仙感遇传 - The title of the source text, suggesting a narrative of divine encounters and spiritual transformations.

[208]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 少落拓不事家产 - Describes a young person who is carefree and does not manage family affairs diligently.
  • 以志气间旷纵酒间游 - Indicates a lifestyle characterized by lofty ideals and frequent drinking and wandering.
  • 资产荡尽 - Means that all assets have been exhausted or depleted.
  • 投于亲故皆以不事事见弃 - Suggests that he was rejected by relatives and friends because he did not engage in productive activities.
  • 衣破腹空 - Describes a state of extreme poverty, where one’s clothes are tattered and there is nothing to eat.
  • 徒行长安中 - Indicates walking barefoot or without proper footwear in the capital city.
  • 日晚未食彷徨不知所往 - Describes a situation of being lost and hungry late in the day.
  • 仰天长吁 - Means to sigh deeply while looking up to the sky.
  • 几缗则丰用 - A question asking how much money would be sufficient for a comfortable life.
  • 三五万则可以活矣 - An answer indicating that three to five million coins would be enough to survive.
  • 未也更言之 - Means 'not yet, say more.'
  • 百万亦曰未也 - Indicates that even a million coins is not enough.
  • 三百万乃曰可矣 - Means that three million coins is finally considered sufficient.
  • 袖出一缗 - Describes the act of taking out a coin from one's sleeve.
  • 给子今夕明日午时候子于西市波斯邸 - A command to meet the next day at noon at a Persian shop in the Western Market.
  • 及时子春往 - Means that Subing went on time.
  • 不告姓名而去 - Indicates that the old man left without revealing his name.
  • 荡心复炽 - Describes a renewed sense of recklessness.
  • 乘肥衣轻 - Indicates a luxurious lifestyle with good food and fine clothing.
  • 会酒徒征丝管歌舞于倡楼 - Describes frequenting brothels and enjoying music and dance.
  • 不复以治生为意 - Means that he no longer cared about managing his wealth.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 周隋间人 - Refers to someone living during the transition period between the Zhou and Sui dynasties.
  • 长安 - The ancient capital of China, now known as Xi'an.
  • 西市 - A market area in the western part of Chang'an.
  • 波斯邸 - A Persian shop, indicating the presence of foreign traders in the market.
  • 杜子春 - A historical or legendary figure, possibly a fictional character in this context.

Proper Nouns

  • 杜子春 - A proper noun referring to the protagonist.
  • 长安 - A proper noun referring to the capital city.
  • 西市 - A proper noun referring to the market area.
  • 波斯邸 - A proper noun referring to the Persian shop.

[209]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 年间稍稍而尽 - This phrase is somewhat ambiguous. It could mean 'gradually over the years' or 'over time in small increments.' The context suggests a gradual process of depletion or change.
  • 衣服车马易贵从贱 - This phrase describes a shift in values, where previously valuable items (clothes, carriages, horses) become less valued, and previously less valued items (donkeys, walking) become more valued.
  • 去马而驴去驴而徒 - This phrase describes a series of changes in transportation, from horses to donkeys and then to walking.
  • 既而复无计自叹于市门 - This phrase means 'after that, he had no plan and lamented at the market gate.'
  • 发声而老人到 - This phrase means 'he shouted, and an old man appeared.'
  • 握其手曰君复如此奇哉 - This phrase means 'he took his hand and said, 'How strange you are again.''
  • 吾复济子几缗方可 - This phrase is somewhat unclear. It could mean 'I will help you with a few strings of coins, but how much do you need?'
  • 子春慙不应 - This phrase means 'Zi Chun was ashamed and did not respond.'
  • 老人因逼之 - This phrase means 'the old man pressed him.'
  • 子春愧谢而已 - This phrase means 'Zi Chun felt ashamed and thanked him.'
  • 明日午时来前期处 - This phrase means 'come to the agreed place tomorrow at noon.'
  • 心又翻然纵适 - This phrase means 'his heart suddenly turned to indulgence.'
  • 不一二年间贫过旧日 - This phrase means 'within a few years, he became poorer than before.'
  • 复遇老人于故处 - This phrase means 'he met the old man again at the same place.'
  • 不胜其愧掩面而走 - This phrase means 'he was overwhelmed with shame and covered his face, running away.'
  • 牵裾止之 - This phrase means 'he pulled his robe to stop him.'
  • 嗟乎拙谋也 - This phrase means 'Alas, what a poor plan!'
  • 因与三千万 - This phrase means 'he gave him three million coins.'
  • 此而不痊则子贫在膏肓矣 - This phrase means 'If this doesn't cure you, your poverty will be incurable.'
  • 吾落拓邪游生涯罄尽 - This phrase means 'My life of wandering and debauchery has been exhausted.'
  • 亲戚豪族无相顾者 - This phrase means 'no relatives or wealthy families care about me.'
  • 独此叟三给我我何以当之 - This phrase means 'only this old man has given me three times, how can I bear it?'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 年间 - Refers to a period of time, often used in historical contexts.
  • 衣服车马 - Refers to clothing, carriages, and horses, which were symbols of wealth and status in ancient China.
  • 驴 - Refers to donkeys, which were a common mode of transportation for the less affluent.
  • 徒 - Refers to walking, the most basic and humble form of transportation.
  • 市门 - Refers to the market gate, a public place where people gather.
  • 缗 - Refers to strings of coins, a unit of currency in ancient China.
  • 落拓邪游 - Refers to a life of wandering and debauchery, often associated with the lower classes or those who have fallen from grace.
  • 亲戚豪族 - Refers to relatives and wealthy families, indicating social connections and support networks.
  • 膏肓 - Refers to a part of the body that is difficult to reach, often used metaphorically to describe an incurable condition.

Proper Nouns

  • 子春 - Refers to Zi Chun, the main character in the story.
  • 老人 - Refers to the old man who helps Zi Chun.

[210]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 矣感叟深惠 - Expresses deep gratitude, meaning 'indeed, I am deeply grateful to the old man.'
  • 立事之後 - Means 'after the establishment of affairs' or 'once matters are settled.'
  • 唯叟所使 - Means 'to do whatever the old man commands.'
  • 吾心也 - Means 'it is my heart' or 'it is my will.'
  • 子治生畢來歲中元見我於老君雙桧下 - A complex sentence meaning 'When you have finished managing your affairs, come to see me at the Double Juniper Pavilion under the old man's will next Midsummer.'
  • 子春以孤孀多寓淮南遂轉資扬州买良田百顷 - Means 'Zi Chun, with many widows and orphans, moved to Huainan and then transferred his assets to Yangzhou to buy a hundred acres of good land.'
  • 郭中起甲第要路置邸百余间 - Means 'in the city, he built a grand mansion and placed over a hundred shops along the main road.'
  • 悉召孤孀分居第中婚嫁甥侄迁袝族亲恩者煦之讐者复之 - Means 'he called all the widows and orphans to live in the mansion, arranged marriages for his nephews and nieces, and moved his relatives. He treated those who were kind to him with warmth and those who had wronged him with forgiveness.'
  • 既毕事及期而往 - Means 'after everything was done, he went at the appointed time.'
  • 老人者方啸于二桧之阴 - Means 'the old man was singing under the shade of the two cypress trees.'
  • 遂与登华山云台峰入四十里余见一处室屋严洁非常人居彩云遥覆鸾飞翔其上 - Means 'and together they ascended Mount Hua to the Cloud Platform Peak, entering over forty miles to see a house that was exceptionally clean and beautiful, covered by distant colorful clouds with phoenixes flying above.'
  • 中有药炉高九尺余紫焰光发灼焕窻戸 - Means 'in the middle of the house, there was a medicine furnace nine feet high, emitting purple flames that illuminated the windows.'
  • 玉女九人环炉而立青龙白虎分据前后 - Means 'nine jade maidens stood around the furnace, with the blue dragon and white tiger standing in front and behind.'
  • 其时日暮老人者不复俗衣乃黄冠绛帔士也 - Means 'at that time, the sun was setting, and the old man no longer wore secular clothes but instead wore a yellow crown and a red robe, like a Taoist.'
  • 持白石三丸酒一巵遗子春令速食之讫取一虎皮铺于内西壁东向而坐戒曰慎勿语虽尊神恶夜叉猛兽地狱及君之亲属为所困缚万 - Means 'he held three white stones and a cup of wine, giving them to Zi Chun, telling him to eat quickly. After finishing, he took a tiger skin and laid it on the west wall, facing east, and warned him, 'Be careful not to speak, even if you encounter great gods, evil spirits, demons, fierce beasts, or hell, and even if your relatives are bound by them.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 中元 - Refers to the Midsummer Festival, a traditional Chinese festival.
  • 老君 - Refers to Laozi, the founder of Taoism.
  • 双桧 - Refers to a pavilion or place named after two cypress trees.
  • 华山 - Refers to Mount Hua, one of the Five Great Mountains of China.
  • 云台峰 - Refers to a peak on Mount Hua.
  • 玉女 - Refers to jade maidens, often associated with Taoist mythology.
  • 青龙白虎 - Refers to the Azure Dragon and White Tiger, symbols of the east and west in Chinese cosmology.
  • 黄冠绛帔 - Refers to the attire of a Taoist priest, with a yellow crown and a red robe.

Proper Nouns

  • 子春 - The name of the protagonist, Zi Chun.
  • 老君 - Refers to Laozi, the founder of Taoism.
  • 华山 - Refers to Mount Hua, one of the Five Great Mountains of China.
  • 云台峰 - Refers to a peak on Mount Hua.
  • 玉女 - Refers to jade maidens, often associated with Taoist mythology.
  • 青龙白虎 - Refers to the Azure Dragon and White Tiger, symbols of the east and west in Chinese cosmology.
  • 黄冠绛帔 - Refers to the attire of a Taoist priest, with a yellow crown and a red robe.

[211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 苦皆非真实 - Philosophical statement suggesting that all suffering is not truly real or is illusory.
  • 宜安心莫惧 - Directive to calm the mind and not fear.
  • 终无所苦 - Outcome of maintaining calm, meaning 'will ultimately suffer nothing.'
  • 当一心念吾所言 - Emphasis on focusing one's mind on what is being said.
  • 言讫而去 - Describes the departure of the speaker after speaking.
  • 子春视庭唯一巨瓮 - Describes Zi Chun looking out and seeing only one large jar.
  • 满中贮水而已 - Describes the contents of the jar, filled with water inside.
  • 道士适去旌旗戈甲千乘万骑 - Describes the sudden appearance of a military force after the departure of the Taoist.
  • 呵叱之声震动天地 - Describes the intensity of the sounds, the shouts and commands shook heaven and earth.
  • 有一人称大军 - Identifies a leader, there was a person calling himself the Great Army.
  • 身长丈余人马皆着金甲光芒射人 - Describes the appearance of the leader and his forces, tall over ten feet, his horses and men all wore golden armor that shone brightly.
  • 直入堂前呵曰 - Describes the bold action of the bodyguards, they entered the hall directly and shouted.
  • 汝是何人敢不避大军 - Asks for the identity of the person, Who are you, and why do you not avoid the Great Army?
  • 又问作何物皆不对 - Indicates the person's silence, they also asked what he was doing, but he did not answer.
  • 问者大怒摧斩争射之声如雷 - Describes the violent reaction, the questioner became furious and began to cut and shoot, the sounds like thunder.
  • 竟不应军者极怒而去 - Describes the final departure, he did not respond, and the Great Army left in extreme anger.
  • 俄而猛虎毒龙狻猊狮子蝮蝎万计哮吼拏攫而争前欲搏噬 - Describes the sudden appearance of dangerous animals, soon, tens of thousands of fierce tigers, venomous dragons, leopards, lions, and scorpions roared and fought to attack and devour.
  • 或跳过其上 - Describes the movement of the animals, some jumped over him.
  • 子春神色不动有顷而散 - Describes his calm reaction, Zi Chun remained unmoved, and after a while, they dispersed.
  • 既而大雨滂澍雷电晦暝 - Describes the weather change, soon, heavy rain fell, and lightning and thunder darkened the sky.
  • 火轮走其左右电光掣其前后目不得开 - Describes the intense lighting, fire wheels moved around him, and lightning flashed before and behind, blinding his eyes.
  • 须臾庭际水深丈余 - Describes the rising water level, soon, the water in the courtyard was over ten feet deep.
  • 流电吼雷势若山川开破不可制止 - Describes the chaotic scene, the lightning flowed, and the thunder roared, as if mountains and rivers were being split apart, unstoppable.
  • 瞬息之间波及坐 - Describes the immediate danger, in an instant, the waves reached him.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 道士 - Refers to a Taoist practitioner, often associated with mystical or supernatural abilities.
  • 旌旗戈甲 - Refers to military banners, spears, and armor, indicating a large and well-equipped army.
  • 千乘万骑 - Refers to a vast number of chariots and horses, symbolizing a powerful military force.
  • 大军 - Refers to a powerful or legendary military leader, possibly a deity or a supernatural being.
  • 金甲 - Refers to golden armor, often associated with divine or heroic figures.
  • 亲卫 - Refers to personal guards or bodyguards, indicating the leader's entourage.
  • 猛虎毒龙狻猊狮子蝮蝎 - Refers to various dangerous animals, often used in classical literature to create a sense of chaos and danger.
  • 大雨滂澍 - Refers to heavy rain, often used to describe a dramatic change in the weather.
  • 雷电晦暝 - Refers to thunder and lightning darkening the sky, often used to create a sense of foreboding or divine intervention.

Proper Nouns

  • 子春 - The name of the person experiencing the events.
  • 大军 - The name or title of the powerful or legendary military leader.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 下子春端坐不頋有顷而散 - Narrative phrase beginning with '下子春' (a name or title) who is depicted as sitting in silence ('端坐不頋') for a while before the scene '散' (disbands or dissolves). '有顷' means 'after a while' or 'after some time.'
  • 大镬汤 - Refers to a large cauldron of boiling liquid, likely used for punishment or execution.
  • 长枪两叉 - A weapon or instrument with a long spear and two prongs, possibly used for torture or execution.
  • 传命曰 - 'Transmitted the command' or 'said' – a phrase used to introduce the voice of an authoritative figure.
  • 肯言姓名即放 - 'If you will state your name, you will be released.'
  • 不肯言即当心叉取 - 'If you will not speak, then you shall be pierced through the heart.'
  • 置之镬中 - 'Place you in the cauldron.'
  • 执其妻来拽于阶下 - 'Took his wife and dragged her down the steps.'
  • 鞭捶流血 - 'Whipped and beaten until bleeding.'
  • 或射或斫或煮或烧 - A list of tortures: 'shot, chopped, boiled, or burned.'
  • 苦不可忍 - 'The suffering was unbearable.'
  • 匍匐拜乞 - 'To crawl and beg.'
  • 但得公一言即全性命矣 - 'If only you would speak one word, your life will be saved.'
  • 雨泪庭中 - 'Cried tears in the courtyard.'
  • 且呪且骂 - 'Cursed and scolded.'
  • 终不頋 - 'Finally did not look' or 'still did not respond.'
  • 吾不能毒汝妻 - 'I cannot poison your wife.'
  • 令取剉碓从脚寸寸剉之 - 'Ordered to bring the millstone and grind her from the feet upward, inch by inch.'
  • 竟不顾之 - 'Still did not look at her.'
  • 此贼妖术已成 - 'This demon’s magic is complete.'
  • 敕左右斩之 - 'Ordered the attendants to behead him.'
  • 魂魄被领见阎罗王 - 'His soul was taken to appear before King Yama.'
  • 捉付 - 'To be captured and handed over.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 子春 - Likely a name or title of a person, possibly a historical or legendary figure. Could be a variant of 'Zichun' or a name from a tale or fable.
  • 大镬汤 - A reference to ancient Chinese execution methods, particularly in the context of judicial or supernatural punishment. The cauldron was often used to boil criminals alive, a practice found in both historical records and folklore.
  • 魂魄被领见阎罗王 - This is a standard description of the soul being taken to the underworld for judgment, a common motif in Chinese Buddhist and Daoist traditions.

Proper Nouns

  • 子春 - A name, possibly fictional or historical, likely a character in a tale or legend.

[213]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 獄中於是镕铜铁杖碓捣硙磨火坑镬汤刀山劒树之苦无不备尝 - Compound phrase listing various forms of torture, including melting copper and iron, pounding with pestles, grinding, fire pits, boiling cauldrons, sword trees, etc. The phrase is a classical way of listing extreme suffering.
  • 心念道士之言亦似可忍 - Thought of the words of the Daoist priest and seemed to be bearable – an idiomatically rendered expression of mental endurance.
  • 竟不呻吟 - 'Yet did not groan' – a concise expression of stoic suffering.
  • 受罪毕 - 'After enduring punishment' – a standard phrase used in classical legal or narrative contexts.
  • 阴贼 - A compound term meaning 'secretly treacherous' or 'hidden malevolence,' often used to describe someone with malicious intent.
  • 不合得作男宜令作女人 - 'Not fit to be a man; better to be a woman' – a moral or divine judgment suggesting a change in gender as punishment.
  • 配生宋州单父县丞王勤家 - 'Assigned to be born into the family of Wang Qin, county magistrate of Danfu, Songzhou' – a classical way of stating a fate or divine assignment.
  • 生而多病针灸药医畧无停日 - 'Born with many illnesses, treated with acupuncture, medicine, and doctors, never a day without treatment' – a common expression in classical biographies of people with chronic illness.
  • 亦尝坠火堕牀痛苦不齐终不失声 - 'Also fell into fire and fell from a bed, experiencing unequal pain, but never lost her voice' – a phrase emphasizing endurance and silence.
  • 容色絶代 - 'Appearance surpassing all' – an idiom used to describe extraordinary beauty.
  • 口无声 - 'Voiceless' – a direct description of a condition.
  • 亲戚狎者侮之万端 - 'Relatives and those who treated her lightly mocked her in countless ways' – an idiomatically rendered expression of persistent mockery.
  • 终不能对 - 'Could never respond' – a concise expression of helplessness.
  • 进士卢珪 - 'Scholar-official Lu Gui' – a standard title for someone who passed the imperial examination.
  • 闻其容而慕之 - 'Hearing of her beauty, he admired her' – a common expression of admiration based on appearance.
  • 因媒氏求焉 - 'Through a matchmaker, he sought her' – a classical way of describing a marriage proposal.
  • 其家以哑辞之 - 'Her family refused on the grounds of her silence' – a standard way of declining a marriage proposal.
  • 亦足以戒长舌之妇 - 'Also serves as a warning to gossipy women' – a proverbial expression.
  • 卢生备六礼亲迎为妻 - 'Lu Gui prepared the six rites and personally married her' – a classical description of a proper wedding.
  • 恩情甚笃 - 'Deep affection' – a standard phrase for strong emotional bond.
  • 生一男仅二岁聪慧无敌 - 'Gave birth to a son, only two years old, with unmatched intelligence' – a common way to describe a gifted child.
  • 卢抱儿与之言不应多方引之终无辞 - 'Lu held the child and spoke to him, but he did not respond; tried many ways to engage him, but still no words' – a narrative expression of a child’s silence.
  • 昔贾大夫之妻鄙其夫才不笑 - 'The wife of Master Jia looked down on her husband’s talent and did not laugh' – a reference to a classical story or allusion.
  • 观其射雉尚释其憾 - 'But when she saw him shoot pheasants, she still let go of her resentment' – a phrase suggesting reconciliation or forgiveness through action.
  • 今吾陋不及贾而文 - 'Now I am inferior to Jia in talent but still am literary' – a self-deprecating comparison.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 狱中 - 'In prison' – a classical setting for narrative.
  • 道士 - 'Daoist priest' – a religious figure in Chinese tradition.
  • 阴贼 - A term used in classical Chinese to describe someone with hidden, malicious intent.
  • 配生 - A term from classical Chinese legal and religious contexts, meaning 'assigned to be born' into a certain family, often used in the context of karma or divine judgment.
  • 宋州单父县丞王勤家 - Refers to a specific family in the Songzhou region, with 'Danfu' as the county and 'Wang Qin' as the county magistrate. This is a historical reference to a real or fictional family in classical Chinese literature.
  • 针灸药医 - Refers to traditional Chinese medicine, including acupuncture and herbal treatments.
  • 进士 - A title for someone who passed the imperial examination in the Tang or Song dynasties.
  • 媒氏 - A matchmaker, a traditional figure in Chinese society.
  • 六礼 - The six traditional rites of marriage in Confucian tradition.
  • 贾大夫 - A reference to a historical or literary figure, possibly from the *Zuo Zhuan* (Commentary of Zuo) or other classical texts, though the specific identity is unclear.
  • 射雉 - A traditional hunting practice, often associated with the aristocracy or scholars.

Proper Nouns

  • 宋州单父县丞王勤 - County Magistrate Wang Qin of Danfu, Songzhou – a proper title and name.
  • 卢珪 - Lu Gui – a proper name of a scholar-official.
  • 贾大夫 - Master Jia – a proper title or name, though the exact identity is uncertain.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 艺非徒射雉也而竟不言大丈夫为妻所鄙安用其子 - A complex sentence combining a general statement about the purpose of an art with a personal anecdote and a moral question: "The art is not merely about hunting pheasants, yet he never mentioned that a great man is despised by his wife; what use is his son?"
  • 乃持两足以头扑于石上应手而碎血溅数步 - Describes a violent action: "He then held his two feet and struck his head against the stone, which shattered upon impact, splattering blood several steps away."
  • 子春爱生于心忽忘其约不觉失声云噫噫声未息身坐故处 - A sequence of actions and emotions: "Zichun's love arose in his heart, and he suddenly forgot his vow, unconsciously exclaiming, 'Ah!' The sound had not yet ceased when he sat in his original place."
  • 道士者亦在其前初五更矣见其紫焰穿屋上大火起四合屋室俱焚 - Describes a scene: "The Taoist was also in front of him. It was early dawn, and he saw the purple flame pierce the roof, a great fire broke out, and the houses and rooms were all consumed by flames."
  • 吾子之心喜怒哀惧恶欲皆忘矣所未臻者爱而已向使子无噫声吾之药成子亦上仙矣 - A philosophical statement: "Your heart has forgotten all joy, anger, sorrow, fear, and desires; the only thing you have not yet achieved is love. If you had not made that sound, my medicine would have been completed, and you would have ascended to immortality."
  • 嗟乎仙才之难得也吾药可重链而子之身犹为世界所容矣勉之哉遥指路使归 - A concluding statement: "Alas, how difficult it is to find a talent for immortality! My medicine can be remade, but your body is still part of this world. Strive hard! He pointed to the road and sent him back."
  • Complex sentences combining general statements with personal anecdotes and moral questions, vivid descriptions of violent actions, sequences of actions and emotions, scenes, philosophical statements, and concluding statements.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大丈夫 - Refers to a noble or virtuous man, often used in classical texts to denote a person of high moral character.
  • 噫噫 - An exclamation of surprise or pain, often used in classical literature to express a sudden emotional reaction.
  • 道士 - Refers to a Taoist priest or practitioner, often associated with spiritual and magical practices.
  • 五更 - Refers to the early morning hours, specifically the first watch of the night, which typically starts around 3 AM.
  • 紫焰 - Purple flame, often associated with supernatural or magical phenomena in classical literature.
  • 上仙 - Refers to a person who has achieved immortality and ascended to heaven, a common theme in Taoist and other Chinese religious texts.
  • Cultural and historical allusions referring to noble or virtuous men, exclamations of surprise or pain, Taoist priests or practitioners, early morning hours, supernatural or magical phenomena, and immortality.

Proper Nouns

  • 子春 - Zichun, a person's name, likely a character in the story.
  • 道士 - Taoist, a term used to refer to a Taoist priest or practitioner.
  • 五更 - The first watch of the night, typically starting around 3 AM.
  • 紫焰 - Purple flame, a term often used in classical literature to describe supernatural or magical phenomena.
  • 上仙 - A person who has achieved immortality and ascended to heaven.
  • Proper nouns including a person's name, a term for a Taoist priest or practitioner, the first watch of the night, a term for a purple flame, and a term for a person who has achieved immortality and ascended to heaven.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 园叟 - A respectful term for a retired gardener.
  • 梁天监中 - During the reign of Emperor Wu of Liang (502–557 CE), specifically during the Tianjian era.
  • 曹掾 - A low-level official in the government, often a clerk or assistant in a bureaucratic office.
  • 秩满 - Completion of an official term of service.
  • 既笄 - A ritual marking a girl's coming of age (typically at 15), indicating she is now eligible for marriage.
  • 召里中媒媪令访良壻 - He summoned a matchmaker from the village to find a good husband for his daughter.
  • 酒阑谓媪曰 - After the wine was finished, he said to the matchmaker...
  • 某诚衰迈灌园之业亦可衣食 - I am truly old and frail, but my work tending the garden provides enough to eat and wear.
  • 幸为求之事成厚谢 - Please help me find a match, and I will reward you handsomely if it succeeds.
  • 媪大骂而去 - The matchmaker scolded him and left.
  • 他日又邀媪 - On another day, he again invited the matchmaker.
  • 叟何不自度岂有衣冠子女肯嫁园叟 - Why don’t you consider yourself? Are there any well-born children who would marry a gardener?
  • 此家诚贫士大夫家之敌者不少顾叟非匹 - This family is indeed poor, and there are many families of scholars and officials who would be a better match than an old gardener.
  • 吾安能为叟一杯酒乃取辱于韦氏 - How could I drink a cup of wine for an old gardener and bring shame upon the Wei family?
  • 叟固曰强为吾一言之言不从即吾命也 - The old man insisted, saying, 'Please speak for me once. If you refuse, it is my fate.'
  • 媪不得已冒责而入言之韦氏大怒 - The matchmaker had no choice but to go in and speak on his behalf. The Wei family was furious.
  • 媪以我贫轻我乃如是且韦家焉有此事况园叟何人敢发 - You think I am poor and look down on me, that’s why you said this. But the Wei family would never do such a thing. What kind of person is this old gardener to make such a claim?

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 梁天监中 - Refers to the Tianjian era (502–557 CE) of the Liang Dynasty, a period of cultural and literary flourishing in southern China.
  • 扬州 - A major city in southern China, historically an important administrative and cultural center.
  • 六合县 - A county in modern-day Jiangsu Province, historically part of Yangzhou.
  • 媒媪 - A matchmaker, typically an older woman who facilitates marriages, especially in rural or traditional settings.
  • 笄 - A ritual for girls coming of age, often at 15, marking their eligibility for marriage.
  • 衣冠子女 - Refers to children of the literati or gentry class, considered 'well-born' or of higher social status.
  • 园叟 - A term of respect for a retired gardener, indicating a humble but respectable profession.
  • 士大夫 - Literally 'scholars and officials,' referring to the educated elite and bureaucratic class in traditional China.
  • 秩满 - Completion of an official term, often leading to retirement or a change in status.

Proper Nouns

  • 张老 - Zhang Lao, a name.
  • 扬州 - Yangzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province.
  • 六合县 - Hexi County, a county in Jiangsu Province.
  • 韦恕 - Wei Shu, a name.
  • 梁天监 - The Tianjian era of the Liang Dynasty (502–557 CE).
  • 扬州曹掾 - A government official in Yangzhou.
  • 媒媪 - A matchmaker, typically a woman.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此议叟固不足责媪何无别之甚 - Concessive statement followed by a rhetorical question: 'This old man's opinion is indeed not worth criticizing, but why is the old woman so unwise?'
  • 诚非所宜言 - Means 'it is truly inappropriate to say,' indicating a sense of propriety or decorum.
  • 为叟所逼不得不达其意 - Means 'being forced by the old man, she could not help but convey his meaning,' suggesting coercion.
  • 韦怒曰 - 'Wei became angry and said,' indicating a shift in tone and a direct speech.
  • 为吾报之令日内得五百缗则可 - Means 'tell him that if he can get 500 strings of cash within the day, it will be acceptable,' indicating a demand.
  • 诚非所宜言为叟所逼不得不达其意 - This phrase is repeated, emphasizing the old woman's predicament.
  • 遂许焉 - Means 'and so agreed,' indicating a decision made.
  • 亲戚恶之亦不能止 - Means 'relatives disapproved but could not stop him,' indicating opposition.
  • 中外之有识者责恕曰君家诚贫乡里岂无贫子弟奈何以女妻园叟既弃之何不令远去也 - Includes a rhetorical question and a statement of criticism.
  • 他日恕致酒召女及张老酒酣微露其意 - 'Later, Shu invited the woman and Zhang Lao to a banquet; when they were drunk, he hinted at his intention.'
  • 恐有留 - Means 'fearing that there might be something left behind,' indicating a concern about unfinished matters.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 园叟 - Refers to an old man who manages a garden, often associated with a lower social status.
  • 园业 - Refers to the garden and its produce, indicating a source of livelihood.
  • 缗 - A unit of currency, typically used in large quantities.
  • 中外之有识者 - Refers to people with insight both inside and outside the family or community.
  • 致酒 - Inviting someone to a banquet, a common social practice.
  • 酒酣 - When people are drunk, often a time for revealing true feelings.
  • 微露其意 - Hinting at one's intention subtly, a polite way of expressing something indirectly.

  • 园叟 - Refers to an old man who manages a garden.
  • 园业 - Refers to the garden and its produce.
  • 缗 - A unit of currency.
  • 中外之有识者 - Refers to people with insight both inside and outside the family or community.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 拜引入防中铺陈之华目所未覩 - Describes the act of being invited into a luxurious and splendid place, where the sights are unprecedented and magnificent.
  • 异香氤氲徧满崖谷 - Describes a pervasive and delightful fragrance filling the valleys and cliffs.
  • 忽闻珠佩之声渐近 - Indicates the sound of jade pendants getting closer, suggesting the presence of someone approaching.
  • 十数青衣容色絶代 - Refers to a group of young women in green attire with extraordinary beauty.
  • 若有所引 - Suggests being guided or led by someone.
  • 戴远游冠衣朱绡曵朱履 - Describes a person wearing a far-traveling hat, red silk clothing, and red shoes.
  • 徐出门一青衣引韦前拜 - Describes a person slowly exiting and being led by a young woman in green attire to bow before someone.
  • 仪状伟然容色芳嫩 - Describes the person's dignified appearance and delicate, fresh complexion.
  • 细视之乃张老也 - Indicates that upon closer inspection, the person is identified as 'Zhang Laoshi' (an old man named Zhang).
  • 人世劳苦若在火中身未清凉愁熖又炽 - Describes the world's labor and troubles as being like being in a fire, with no relief from the heat and constant worry.
  • 无斯须防时兄久客寄何以自娱 - Asks how the brother, who has been away for a long time, can entertain himself.
  • 因揖令坐 - Describes the act of bowing and inviting someone to sit.
  • 未几一青衣来曰娘子已梳头毕 - Indicates that soon a young woman in green attire arrives and says that the lady has finished combing her hair.
  • 遂引入见妹于堂前 - Describes the process of being led to meet the sister in front of the hall.
  • 其堂沉香为梁玳瑁帖门碧玉窻珍珠箔阶砌皆冷滑碧色不辨其物 - Describes the hall with a beam made of agarwood, a door decorated with tortoiseshell, green jade windows, and pearl screens, with the steps and tiles being cold, smooth, and blue, making it hard to identify the material.
  • 其妹服饰之盛世间未见 - Describes the sister's attire as being extremely luxurious and not seen in the world.
  • 有顷进馔精美芳馨不可名状 - Describes the food being served as being exquisite and fragrant, beyond description.
  • 食讫 - Indicates that the meal has ended.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '館韋於內防明日方曙' is elliptical and can be interpreted in multiple ways. It could mean 'hosting Wei at the inner defense, the next day just before dawn' or 'accommodating Wei at the inner guard, the next morning just as dawn breaks.'
  • The sentence '宅中有客安得暮归' is elliptical and can be read as 'there is a guest in the house, how can he return at dusk?' or 'there is a guest in the house, he cannot return at dusk.'
  • The long sequence '俄而五云起于庭中鸾凤飞翔丝竹并作' must be read as a single descriptive passage, indicating a sudden and magical event.
  • The sequence '渐上空中正东而去望之已没犹闻音乐之声' describes the gradual ascent and departure of the group, followed by the lingering sound of music.
  • The long sentence '独居大寂寞然此地神仙之府非俗人得游以兄宿命合得到此然亦不可久居明日当奉别耳' must be read as a single unit, indicating the loneliness of living alone, the exclusivity of the place, the predestined fate of the brother, and the impending departure.

  • 戸栋梁饰以异寳 - Describes the opulence and grandeur of the interior, literally 'the windows, doors, beams, and rafters are adorned with rare treasures.'
  • 屏帐皆画云 - Describes the decorative elements of the interior, which are rich and symbolic, literally 'the screens and curtains are all painted with clouds and dragons.'
  • 四青衣捧碧玉台盘而至 - Describes the arrival of a servant bringing a valuable tray, literally 'four young women in green robes carried a jade tray.'
  • 器物珍异皆非人世所有 - Emphasizes the otherworldly nature of the items, literally 'the vessels and objects are all rare and precious, not found in the mortal world.'
  • 香醪嘉目所未窥 - Emphasizes the exotic and luxurious nature of the feast, literally 'the fragrant wine and delicious food are sights that have never been seen before.'
  • 既而日暮命其促席燃九光之灯光华满坐 - Describes the transition to a more intimate and illuminated setting, literally 'as the sun set, he ordered the seating to be brought closer and the nine-ray lamps to be lit, filling the room with light.'
  • 女乐二十人皆绝代之色列坐其前 - Describes the presence of beautiful and talented musicians, literally 'twenty female musicians, all of extraordinary beauty, sat in front of him.'
  • 顾小黄头曰王评事者吾山中之友道情不固弃吾下山别近十年 - Introduces the friend and the duration of their separation, literally 'he turned to the young boy and said, 'Wang Pingshi, my friend from the mountains, has been away from me for nearly ten years.'
  • 才为廷尉属今俗心已就须俗妓以乐之 - Describes the friend's recent career advancement and his current state of mind, literally 'he has just become an assistant to the Imperial Censor, and now his mind is set on worldly pleasures.'
  • 顾伶家女无足召者当召士大夫之女已适人者如近无姝丽五千里内皆可择之 - Describes the selection process for the entertainment, literally 'there is no need to call on the daughters of courtesans, but we should call on the daughters of scholars and officials who have already married, or if there are no beautiful women nearby, we can choose from within a five-thousand-mile radius.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sequence '薛二人女乐四十余軰拜坐奏乐选女妓十軰同饮有一箜篌妓最为姝颖崔与并坐崔见箜篌上有十字云天际识归舟云间辨江树崔黙记之席散薛问崔坐中所悦以箜篌者对薛曰他日与君今且未可及明与崔送别遗金三十斤送至官路惨别而去' must be read as a series of events leading up to the main narrative.
  • The long sequence '崔至官月余求婚得柳氏常疑曽识而不记其处暇日命取箜篌理曲崔见十字书在焉问其故云某时患疾梦中见使人追云西城大仙陈溪薛君有客五百里内解音声处女尽追可四十余人因随去与薛及客崔少府同饮一夕觉来疾已愈薛君即神仙也崔少府风貌与君无异各话其事大为惊骇方知薛已得道尔' must be read as a series of events leading to the revelation of Xue's divine nature.

Over several years, he traveled throughout Jiangnan, unknown to anyone. Zhao Guizhen often met with Zang Ji's disciple, the Daoist Ye Tongwei of Jiuhua Mountain, and learned the truth. Zhao Guizhen reported Zang Ji's strange abilities to the emperor, who sent a messenger with a hand-edict to urgently summon him. When Zang Ji arrived, he suddenly disappeared on the way. The messenger, frightened, immediately submitted a report detailing the incident. The emperor, upon reading the report, sighed and said, 'I am not as fortunate as Emperor Ming and his successors in meeting extraordinary people.' Later, someone saw Zang Ji sailing a small boat on the sea. To this day, the story is widely told among the Daoist practitioners of Jiangnan. [From *Du Yang Zabian*]'.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sentence '李林甫年二十尚未读书在东都好游猎打毬驰逐鹰狗每于城下槐坛下骑驴击毬略无休日' describes Li Linfu's lifestyle and activities, with multiple clauses that need to be parsed carefully.
  • The sentence '既惫舍驴以两手返据地歇一日有道士甚丑陋见李公踞地徐言曰此有何乐郎君如此爱也' introduces a hermit who criticizes Li Linfu's lifestyle, with a shift in tone and subject.
  • The sentence '李公幼聪悟意其异人乃摄衣起谢道士曰郎君虽善此然忽有颠坠之苦则悔不可及' shows Li Linfu's response to the hermit, with a shift in focus to the hermit's warning.
  • The sequence '李公请自此修谨不复为也道士笑曰与郎君后三日五更防于此曰诺及往道士已先至曰为约何后李乃谢之曰更三日复来' describes the interaction between Li Linfu and the hermit, with a promise to meet again.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 槐坛所覩也慙悸之极若无所措 - Describes an overwhelming sense of shame and fear, to the point of being at a loss. The imagery of '槐坛' (locust tree altar) suggests a place of reverence or solemnity, which amplifies the emotional intensity.
  • 却思二十年之事今已至矣 - Reflects on past events (specifically, events from twenty years ago) that have now come to fruition. The use of '却思' (reflecting back) adds a layer of introspection.
  • 所承教戒曽不蹔行 - Indicates that the teachings or warnings received have never been temporarily followed, suggesting a complete disregard for them.
  • 中心如疾 - Means that one's inner state is in turmoil, as if suffering from a disease. It conveys a deep sense of distress or agitation.
  • 乃拜道士迎笑曰 - Describes the act of bowing to a Taoist priest and greeting them with a smile, indicating a formal and respectful interaction.
  • 相公安否 - A polite inquiry about the well-being of the person being addressed, commonly used in classical Chinese to show respect.
  • 遣相公行隂徳 - Means that the person in question was sent to perform good deeds secretly. '隂徳' (secret virtue) refers to acts of kindness done without seeking recognition.
  • 枉杀人 - Means 'to kill in vain' or 'to kill unjustly,' implying a wrongful act that has consequences.
  • 上天甚明谴谪可畏 - Suggests that the heavens are clear in their judgment and that divine punishment is to be feared. '谴谪' (punishment) implies a retribution from the heavens.
  • 如何 - A rhetorical question used to express a plea for help or guidance.
  • 某人间之数将满 - Suggests that the person's earthly lifespan is coming to an end. '人间之数' (the number of days in the mortal world) refers to one's allotted time on Earth.
  • 莫要知否亦可一行 - Suggests that the person should not inquire further, as it might lead to unnecessary worry, but they can still go ahead with their plans.
  • 相公安神静虑万想俱遣 - Means that the person should calm their mind, focus their thoughts, and let go of all distractions. '遣' (let go) implies a release from mental burdens.
  • 兀如枯株 - Describes a state of being as still and unresponsive as a dried-up tree stump, symbolizing a detached and empty mind.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 槐坛 - A locust tree altar, often associated with rituals or places of reverence in ancient China. The mention of '槐坛' suggests a setting where important events or decisions take place.
  • 道士 - A Taoist priest, who plays a significant role in Chinese religious and philosophical traditions. The interaction with the Taoist priest indicates a spiritual or mystical element in the narrative.
  • 隂徳 - Secret virtue, referring to good deeds performed without seeking recognition. This concept is central to Taoist and Confucian ethics, emphasizing the importance of humility and selflessness.
  • 上天 - The heavens, often personified as a divine force that judges human actions. The idea of '上天' is deeply rooted in Chinese cosmology and belief in karma.
  • 人间之数 - The number of days in the mortal world, referring to one's allotted lifespan. This concept reflects the belief in predestined fate and the limited nature of human existence.
  • 遣 - The act of sending someone to perform a task, often with a moral or spiritual purpose. In this context, it refers to being sent to do good deeds.

  • 道士 - The term for a Taoist priest, which should be consistently translated as 'Taoist priest' or 'Daoist priest' throughout the text.

  • 具以告道士曰酒是灵物必得入口当换其骨泻之不出亦乃命也 - Elliptical. Can be read as 'The servant reported everything to the Taoist priest, who said, 'The wine is a sacred substance, it must be drunk to change its essence. If it won't pour out, it's fate.'
  • 与栗吃俄一人于筵上取二栗付侍者与可交令便吃 - Elliptical. Can be read as 'To give chestnuts to eat, one person took two chestnuts from the table and gave them to the servant to give to Wang Kejiao to eat.'
  • 视之其栗青赤光如枣长二寸许囓之有皮非人间之栗肉脆而甘如饴久之食方尽 - Long descriptive phrase. Can be read as 'Looking at it, the chestnuts had a greenish-red light like a date, about two inches long. Biting it had a skin, not an earthly chestnut. The meat was crisp and sweet like honey. After a long time, it was finally finished eating.'
  • 王可交已见之矣可令去命一黄衣送上岸于船边覔所乘渔舟不见 - Elliptical. Can be read as 'Wang Kejiao has already seen it. He can be let go. Ordered a yellow-robed person to take it ashore, looked for the fishing boat he had ridden on the shore but couldn't find it.'
  • 合眼自到于是合眼若风水林木浩浩之声令开眼已到失黄衣所在 - Elliptical. Can be read as 'Close your eyes and you'll arrive. So he closed his eyes, and it felt like the sound of wind, water, and trees. When he opened his eyes, he had arrived, but the yellow-robed person was gone.'
  • 但见峰峦重叠松栢参天坐于草中石上及望见有门楼人出入俄顷采樵者并僧十余人到问 - Long descriptive phrase. Can be read as 'He only saw mountains overlapping, pine and cypress trees reaching up to the sky, sitting on grass and stones. Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The opening sequence '衣黄再拜于前已而奏曰' describes the formal act of bowing and speaking, which must be read as a single, cohesive action.
  • The clause '臣孙思邈也庐于峨眉山有年矣' introduces the speaker and his background, emphasizing his long-term residence in Mount Emei.
  • The clause '今闻銮驾幸成都臣故候谒' explains the reason for the meeting, linking the emperor's arrival to the speaker's presence.
  • The request '愿以八十两为赐脱遂臣请幸降使赍至峨眉山' is a formal and detailed plea for a gift and its delivery.
  • The sequence '悸然而寤即诏侍臣陈忠盛挈雄黄八十两往峨眉宣赐思邈' describes the emperor's response and the immediate action taken to fulfill the request.
  • The clause '见一叟貌甚俊古衣黄立于岭下谓忠盛曰' introduces the appearance and actions of the elderly man, setting the stage for the interaction.
  • The exchange '汝非天子使乎我即孙思邈也' confirms the identity of the elderly man and the envoy.
  • The final clause '吾蒙天子赐雄黄今有表谢属山居无翰墨天使命笔札传写以进也' explains the need for writing materials and the request for the emperor to provide them.

  • 自是或隐或见 - Means 'from then on, sometimes appearing and sometimes disappearing.'
  • 父母以其好善使于白水僧院为童子 - '父母以其好善' means 'his parents because of his goodness,' '使于白水僧院' means 'sent him to a temple in Baisui,' and '为童子' means 'as a young monk.'
  • 曰有游客称孙处士周游院中讫袖中出汤末以授童子 - '曰' means 'said,' '有游客称孙处士' means 'a traveler called himself Mr. Sun,' '周游院中' means 'roamed the temple,' '讫袖中出汤末' means 'finally took out some powdered tea from his sleeve,' and '以授童子' means 'and gave it to the young monk.'
  • 为我如茶法煎来 - Means 'please brew it according to the tea method.'
  • 处士呷少许以余汤与之 - '处士呷少许' means 'Mr. Sun tasted a little bit,' and '以余汤与之' means 'and gave the remaining tea to him.'
  • 觉汤极美愿赐一碗 - '觉汤极美' means 'found the tea extremely delicious,' and '愿赐一碗' means 'wished to be given a bowl.'
  • 处士曰此汤为汝来耳 - '处士曰' means 'Mr. Sun said,' '此汤为汝来耳' means 'this tea was made for you.'
  • 即以末方寸匕更令煎吃 - '即以末方寸匕' means 'then took a small amount of the powder,' and '更令煎吃' means 'and asked him to brew and drink it again.'
  • 因与同侣话之出门处士已去矣 - '因与同侣话之' means 'then talked with his companions,' '出门处士已去矣' means 'when they went out, Mr. Sun had already left.'
  • 童子亦乘空而飞 - Means 'the young monk also flew into the air.'
  • 众方惊异顾视煎汤铫子已成金矣 - '众方惊异' means 'everyone was surprised,' '顾视煎汤铫子' means 'looked at the tea pot,' and '已成金矣' means 'which had turned into gold.'

  • 每过午必至其日暨夜都不到 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'every day after noon, they would arrive, but by nightfall, they would not be there,' indicating a pattern of arrival and departure.
  • 莫是罗君否 - Colloquial expression meaning 'is it Lord Luo?' used to ask for confirmation.
  • 时天寒围炉 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'it was cold, and they were huddling around a fire,' indicating a scene of warmth.
  • 遂至叶诘使者云欲到京焰火亘天无路可过 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'they then went to Ye to question the messenger, who said they wanted to go to the capital, but the flames reached the sky, and there was no way to pass,' indicating a challenging situation.
  • 适火歇方得度 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'just as the fire subsided, they were able to cross,' indicating a moment of relief.
  • 从此众皆敬伏 - Formal expression meaning 'from then on, everyone respected and submitted to him,' indicating a change in attitude.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 歩而灭且召伶官依其声调作霓裳羽衣曲 - A sequence of actions: walking, summoning court musicians, and composing the 'Feather Dress and Cloud Cap' melody based on its rhythm.
  • 武惠妃尤信金刚三藏 - '尤信' means 'especially trusted,' indicating a high level of confidence in the person mentioned.
  • 顾谓三藏曰 - '顾谓' means 'looked and said,' a formal way of indicating a direct address.
  • 上人能致此乎 - '上人' is a respectful term for a monk or master, and '能致此乎' means 'can you achieve this?' The phrase is a polite inquiry.
  • 此幻化耳 - '幻化' means 'illusion' or 'transformation,' indicating that the object is not real.
  • 袖中出七寳如意以进 - '袖中出' means 'took out from his sleeve,' a common gesture in classical Chinese to indicate producing something mysteriously.
  • 公逺所进者即时化为竹枝耳 - '即时化为' means 'immediately transformed into,' emphasizing the magical nature of the event.
  • 吾方闲闷可试小法以为乐也 - '闲闷' means 'bored,' and '可试小法以为乐也' means 'can try a small trick for amusement.'
  • 师试为朕举此方木 - '试为' means 'try to do,' and '举此方木' means 'lift this beam.'
  • 师之神力何其失耶 - '神力' means 'divine power,' and '何其失耶' means 'why has it failed?'
  • 三藏使金刚善神众压一头故不举 - '使' means 'caused,' '金刚善神众' means 'Buddhist deities,' and '压一头故不举' means 'pressed down one end so it wouldn't lift.'
  • 武妃宗释武妃颇有悦色 - '宗释' means 'Buddhist,' and '颇有悦色' means 'showed a pleased expression.'
  • 三藏亦隂心自懽 - '隂心' means 'secretly,' and '自懽' means 'rejoiced.'
  • 惟公逺低头微哂 - '惟' means 'only,' '低头' means 'lowered his head,' and '微哂' means 'smiled slightly.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 之叶禹歩叩齿绕三匝曰 - Describes a ritualistic action where a person (叶禹歩) performs a specific gesture (叩齿绕三匝), which likely has symbolic or magical significance.
  • 太上老君摄去盆下袈裟之缕随色皆摄各为一聚 - Refers to a magical act where the deity 太上老君 (Taisang Laojun) gathers the threads of a robe (袈裟) by color, each forming a separate pile. The term '摄去' suggests a supernatural action of collecting or gathering.
  • 惜哉金襴至毁如此 - Expresses regret over the destruction of something valuable (金襴, a type of precious fabric).

  • 烧之烘赤手捧以合三藏头失声而走 - Describes a dramatic scene where something is burned, and the heat causes someone (三藏) to cry out and run away.
  • 末法也 - Refers to the 'last age' or 'degenerate age' in Buddhist terminology, suggesting a decline in spiritual purity.
  • 何用逞之 - Means 'why use it to show off' or 'why display it.'
  • 师不能为朕作一术以惧朕耶 - Asks if the teacher (师) can perform a magical act to frighten the emperor (朕).
  • 请更问三藏法术何如 - Suggests that the emperor wants to know more about the monk's (三藏) magical abilities.
  • 贫道请收固袈裟试令罗公取取不得则罗公输取得则僧输 - Describes a test where the monk (贫道) asks 罗公 (Luogong) to retrieve the robe (袈裟), with the condition that if he fails, he loses, and if he succeeds, the monk loses.
  • 结坛焚香自于坛上跏趺作法取袈裟贮之银合又安数重木函皆有封锁置于坛上 - Describes a ritual where the monk sets up an altar, burns incense, and performs a magical act to retrieve the robe, placing it in a silver box and several wooden boxes with locks.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 尊四海之贵宗庙之重社稷之大 - Formal expression emphasizing reverence for the empire, ancestral temples, and the state; can be translated as 'revering the vast empire, the sacred ancestral temples, and the great state.'
  • 怀玺入人家困于鱼服 - Metaphorical expression where '怀玺' (wearing the imperial seal) and '鱼服' (wearing fish-like attire) symbolize the constraints of being an emperor; can be translated as 'being burdened by the responsibilities of being an emperor.'
  • 托身隐常有不尽或露裾带或见影迹 - Description of the figure's partial invisibility; can be translated as 'his partial invisibility was not complete, sometimes revealing his skirt or shadow.'
  • 坚学隐形之术强之不已因而教焉 - Description of the figure teaching the art of invisibility; can be translated as 'insisting on learning the art of invisibility, he could not be dissuaded and thus taught him.'
  • 天师云水适意岂不念内殿相识耶 - Rhetorical question asking if the figure does not remember their acquaintance in the inner palace; can be translated as 'Master, you are at ease with the clouds and water, but don't you remember our acquaintance in the inner palace?'
  • 栖息林泉以修真为务自晋咸和年入蜀访师 - Description of the figure's lifestyle and mission; can be translated as 'he dwells in the mountains and streams, devoting himself to cultivating the true self, entering Sichuan in the Jianhe year of the Jin dynasty to seek a teacher.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 诸山久晦名迹 - A phrase meaning 'the mountains have long concealed their famous traces,' suggesting a period of obscurity or hiding.
  • 乃舍烟霞放旷之乐 - Means 'thus abandoning the joy of wandering in mist and clouds,' referring to a life of seclusion and freedom.
  • 冒尘世腥羶之路 - Means 'venturing into the stinking and膻 (膻 refers to the smell of animal fat) path of the mundane world,' describing the act of entering a polluted or impure environment.
  • 混迹鸡防之羣 - Means 'mixing among the crowd of chickens and pigs,' a metaphor for mingling with ordinary people.
  • 窥阅蜉蝣之境 - Means 'glimpsing the world of the mayfly,' a metaphor for a short-lived or transient existence.
  • 不以为倦者 - Means 'not tiring of it,' indicating a persistent effort despite difficulties.
  • 盖欲以至道之贵俯教于人主耳 - Means 'it is because they wish to teach the supreme Dao to the ruler,' expressing the ultimate goal of their actions.
  • 圣上延我于别殿 - Means 'the emperor invited me to a private hall,' indicating a special meeting.
  • 遽以灵药为索 - Means 'immediately sought me with divine medicine,' suggesting a sudden and urgent request.
  • 我告以人间之腑脏荤血充积三田未虚六气未洁 - Means 'I informed him that my inner organs were filled with meat and blood, and the three fields were not empty, and the six energies were not pure,' explaining my condition.
  • 不能守此诫约加我以丹颈之戮 - Means 'if I cannot adhere to this admonition, I will be punished by the red neck,' indicating a severe consequence.
  • 一何遑遽哉 - Means 'how anxious and hurried is this,' expressing a sense of urgency and concern.
  • 然得道之人与道气混合岂可以世俗兵刃水火害于我哉 - Means 'but those who have attained the Dao and are mixed with the Dao cannot be harmed by worldly weapons and fire,' indicating the invulnerability of the Daoist.
  • 但念主上列丹华之籍有玉京交契之旧躬欲度之眷眷之情不能巳巳 - Means 'but thinking of the emperor's list of the red flowers and the old friendship with the Jade Capital, I personally wish to guide him, and my affection cannot be stopped,' expressing a deep emotional connection.
  • 因袖中出书一缄谓仙玉曰可以此上闻云 - Means 'I then took out a letter from my sleeve and said to Xian Yu, 'You can present this to the emperor,' indicating a specific action.
  • 我姓维名厶远静真先生弟子也 - Means 'My name is Wei, and I am a disciple of the far-silent true master,' introducing myself.
  • 上必寤焉言罢而去 - Means 'The emperor will surely awaken; I then left,' indicating the expected outcome and my departure.
  • 仍以蜀当归为寄遂失所在 - Means 'I also gave him Sichuan Angelica to carry with him, and then I disappeared,' indicating a symbolic gift and my disappearance.
  • 仙玉还京师以事及所寄之缄奏焉 - Means 'Xian Yu returned to the capital and reported the matter and the letter he carried,' indicating the reporting of the events.

  • 三峰歌八首 - Refers to a set of eight songs about the Three Peaks.

  • 家于光州乐安县黄土山 - 'Lived in Huangtu Mountain, Le'an County, Guangzhou,' a typical way to describe one's residence.

  • 衣服饮食如常人 - 'Clothing and food were like those of ordinary people,' indicating a normal lifestyle despite his unusual occupation.
  • 卖药为业 - 'Made a living by selling medicine,' a common occupation in ancient times.
  • 开元三年前 - 'Before the third year of the Kaiyuan era,' referring to a specific period during the Tang Dynasty.

  • 先生过之 - 'The master passed by,' indicating a visit.
  • 防命男弁为主善待之 - 'Wang Fang ordered his son Bian to treat the master well,' showing hospitality.
  • 授以杏丹术 - 'Taught him the art of preparing the apricot elixir,' indicating a transfer of knowledge.
  • 乘云而度 - 'Rode on clouds and passed through,' a metaphor for supernatural abilities.
  • 仰告曰 - 'Looked up and said,' indicating a respectful address.
  • 教弁丹术未成奈何舍我而去 - 'Taught Bian the elixir-making technique but it was not yet complete, why leave me now,' expressing regret and concern.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 诣休光府 - Visit the office of Xiu Guang, where '诣' means 'to visit' or 'to go to,' and '府' refers to an official's office or residence.
  • 踞见且诟曰 - Sat down and scolded, saying, where '踞见' means 'sat down to see' (a gesture of disrespect), and '诟' means 'scold' or 'reproach.'
  • 若仙当遂往矣 - If you are a fairy, you should have left already, where '遂' means 'already' or 'definitely,' and '往' means 'to go away.'
  • 今去而复来妖也 - Now you leave and come back, you are an evil spirit, where '复' means 'again' or 'back,' and '妖' means 'evil spirit' or 'demon.'
  • 余説之未毕故止 - I was not finished explaining, so I stayed, where '説' means 'to explain' or 'to talk,' and '故' means 'so' or 'therefore.'
  • 执之 - Seize him, where '执' means 'to seize' or 'to arrest.'
  • 龙虎见于侧 - Dragons and tigers appeared beside him, where '见' means 'to appear' or 'to be seen.'
  • 乘之而去 - Rode them away, where '乘' means 'to ride' or 'to mount.'
  • 丈余 - Over a foot, where '丈' is a unit of measurement (approximately 3.3 meters), and '余' means 'more than.'
  • 斯须雷电大至 - Soon, thunder and lightning came in great force, where '斯须' means 'soon' or 'in a short time,' and '大至' means 'came in great force.'
  • 碎庭槐十余株 - Broke over a dozen courtyard hawthorns, where '碎' means 'to break,' and '十余' means 'over ten.'
  • 府舎皆震坏 - The official residence was all shaken and damaged, where '震坏' means 'shaken and damaged.'
  • 观者无不奔溃 - All the onlookers ran away in panic, where '奔溃' means 'ran away in panic.'
  • 惧而走 - Fled in fear, where '惧' means 'fear,' and '走' means 'to run away.'
  • 失头巾 - Lost his headscarf, where '失' means 'to lose,' and '头巾' means 'headscarf' or 'turban.'
  • 引妻子跣出府 - Led his wife and children out of the office barefoot, where '引' means 'to lead,' '妻子' means 'wife and children,' and '跣' means 'barefoot.'
  • 徙宅焉 - Moved his residence there, where '徙' means 'to move,' and '宅' means 'residence.'
  • 改乐安县为仙居县 - Changed Le'an County to Xianju County, where '改' means 'to change,' and '县' means 'county.'
  • 就先生所居舎置仙堂观 - Built Xiantang Temple at the place where the master lived, where '就' means 'at,' '所居' means 'where he lived,' and '观' means 'temple' or 'monastery.'
  • 黄土村为仙堂村 - Changed Huangtu Village to Xiantang Village, where '黄土' means 'yellow earth,' and '村' means 'village.'
  • 县尉严正诲防营筑焉 - The county magistrate Yan Zheng supervised the construction, where '县尉' means 'county magistrate,' '严正' is the name, '诲防' means 'supervised,' and '营筑' means 'construction.'
  • 度王弁为观主兼谏议大夫 - Appointed Wang Bian as the abbot of the temple and also as a censor, where '度' means 'appointed,' '观主' means 'abbot,' and '谏议大夫' means 'censor.'
  • 号通真先生 - Called him Master Tongzhen, where '号' means 'called' or 'named,' and '通真' means 'Master Tongzhen.'
  • 饵杏丹却老 - Took apricot elixir to delay aging, where '饵' means 'to take,' '杏丹' means 'apricot elixir,' and '却老' means 'to delay aging.'
  • 筋力称是 - His strength matched his appearance, where '筋力' means 'strength,' and '称是' means 'matched' or 'corresponded to.'
  • 其后果州女子谢自然白日上升 - Later, a woman from Gaozhou named Xie Ziran ascended to heaven during the day, where '后果' means 'later,' '州' means 'state' or 'province,' '女子' means 'woman,' '谢自然' is the name, and '白日上升' means 'ascended to heaven during the day.'
  • 当自然学道时 - When Xie Ziran was practicing Daoism, where '当' means 'when,' '学道' means 'practicing Daoism.'
  • 神仙频降 - Spirits frequently descended, where '神仙' means 'spirits' or 'immortals,' and '频降' means 'frequently descended.'
  • 有姓崔者亦云名崔 - There was a man surnamed Cui who also said his name was Cui, where '有' means 'there was,' '姓崔者' means 'a man surnamed Cui,' and '亦云名崔' means 'also said his name was Cui.'
  • 有姓杜者亦云名杜 - There was a man surnamed Du who also said his name was Du, where '有' means 'there was,' '姓杜者' means 'a man surnamed Du,' and '亦云名杜' means 'also said his name was Du.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 诣休光府休光踞见且诟曰 - This phrase is elliptical and must be read as 'Visited the office of Xiu Guang, Xiu Guang sat down and scolded, saying.'
  • 若仙当遂往矣今去而复来妖也 - This phrase is elliptical and must be read as 'If you are a fairy, you should have left already. Now you leave and come back, you are an evil spirit.'
  • 余説之未毕故止非他也 - This phrase is elliptical and must be read as 'I was not finished explaining, so I stayed. It was not because of anything else.'
  • 执之龙虎见于侧先生乘之而去 - This phrase is elliptical and must be read as 'Seized him. Dragons and tigers appeared beside him. The master rode them away.'
  • 丈余玄云四合斯须雷电大至 - This phrase is elliptical and must be read as 'Over a foot, dark clouds gathered all around. Soon, thunder and lightning came in great force.'

  • 言讫有五色云自北方来 - Vivid description of 'after speaking, a colorful cloud came from the north,' indicating the arrival of the deity.

  • 县官再拜问其姓氏 - Formal phrase meaning 'the county official bowed twice and asked for his name,' showing respect and curiosity.

  • 一脚着靴一脚跣 - A unique and symbolic way of dressing, often associated with Daoist immortals who transcend conventional norms.
  • 行夏则衫内加絮 - Reflects his ability to adapt to different seasons, symbolizing his mastery over natural elements.

  • 持大拍板长三尺余 - The large clappers are a symbol of his musical and performative nature.
  • 常醉歌老少皆随看之 - His ability to attract a wide audience, both young and old, reflects his universal appeal.
  • 人问应声答之笑皆絶倒 - His ability to respond to questions with humor and make people laugh is a sign of his wisdom and charm.
  • 行则振靴言歌歌 - His habit of shaking his boots and singing while walking is a symbolic act of liberation and joy.
